channel.title: Latte on Barista At Home
channel.description: Recent content in Latte on Barista At Home

item.title: How to Steam Milk for Espresso Drinks: A Beginner's Guide to Microfoam
item.description: Master milk steaming for lattes, cappuccinos, and flat whites. Step-by-step technique, temperature guide (140–155°F), milk types compared, and the mistakes that trip up every beginner.

item.title: Americano vs. Latte: What's the Difference?
item.description: An Americano is espresso and hot water — no milk. A latte is espresso and steamed milk. Here's how they compare on flavor, caffeine, calories, and when to choose each.

item.title: Espresso vs. Latte: What's the Difference?
item.description: Espresso is a small concentrated coffee shot. A latte is espresso combined with steamed milk. Here's how they compare on size, flavor, caffeine, and calories.

item.title: Latte vs Coffee: What's the Difference?
item.description: Latte vs coffee: a latte is espresso plus steamed milk; drip coffee is brewed with hot water alone. Compare taste, caffeine, calories, and when to choose each.

item.title: Latte vs Mocha: Key Differences Explained
item.description: Latte vs mocha: a latte is espresso + steamed milk; a mocha adds chocolate sauce. Compare taste, calories, caffeine, and when to choose each.

item.title: Espresso Drink Calories: Latte, Cappuccino, Americano & More
item.description: How many calories are in a latte, cappuccino, americano, or espresso? Calorie counts for every popular espresso drink by size, milk type, and add-ins — with a quick-reference table.

item.title: Gingerbread Latte (with Homemade Gingerbread Syrup)
item.description: Make a gingerbread latte at home with a simple homemade gingerbread syrup — ground ginger, cinnamon, cloves, and brown sugar. Works hot or iced, year-round.

item.title: Maple Latte (Hot or Iced, with Real Maple Syrup)
item.description: A maple latte is espresso + steamed milk + real maple syrup — naturally sweet without a cloying aftertaste. Learn the right ratio, which maple syrup to use, and how to make it hot or iced.

item.title: What Is a Latte? Espresso, Milk Ratios, and Types Explained
item.description: A latte is espresso topped with steamed milk and a thin layer of microfoam. Learn what makes a latte, how ratios work, types of lattes, and how it differs from a cappuccino or flat white.

item.title: Black Sesame Latte: How to Make It at Home (Hot, Iced, and Dirty)
item.description: A black sesame latte is a rich, nutty espresso drink made with black sesame paste and steamed milk. Learn how to make hot, iced, and dirty espresso versions at home.

item.title: Coconut Latte: How to Make It at Home (Hot, Iced, and Toasted)
item.description: A coconut latte combines espresso with coconut milk or coconut syrup for a tropical, lightly sweet coffee drink. Learn the 3 best methods — coconut milk, coconut syrup, and toasted coconut.

item.title: Horchata Latte Recipe (Iced + Hot — With Homemade Rice Milk)
item.description: How to make a horchata latte at home — espresso shot pulled over homemade cinnamon rice milk. Iced and hot versions, plus quick method with store-bought horchata.

item.title: Taro Latte: How to Make It at Home (Hot, Iced, and Dirty)
item.description: A taro latte is a creamy, mildly sweet purple drink made from taro root. Learn how to make hot, iced, and dirty espresso taro lattes at home — with taro paste or powder.

item.title: Cortado vs Latte: What's the Difference?
item.description: A cortado uses equal parts espresso and steamed milk (1:1). A latte uses 5–6x more milk than espresso. Here's the full comparison including size, strength, foam, and when to order each.

item.title: Spanish Latte Recipe: Creamy Condensed Milk Coffee (Hot & Iced)
item.description: Make a Spanish latte at home — espresso + condensed milk + steamed milk. Includes hot and iced versions, exact ratios, and tips for the perfect sweetness balance.

item.title: Brown Sugar Latte Recipe
item.description: Make a brown sugar latte at home — a warm, caramel-sweet espresso drink with steamed milk and homemade brown sugar syrup. Hot and iced versions included.

item.title: Cinnamon Dolce Latte Recipe (Starbucks Copycat)
item.description: Make a cinnamon dolce latte at home with a simple homemade cinnamon dolce syrup — hot or iced, with Starbucks copycat ratios included.

item.title: Lavender Latte Recipe (Hot & Iced)
item.description: Make a lavender latte at home with homemade lavender syrup — step-by-step instructions for hot and iced versions, plus Starbucks copycat ratios.

item.title: Matcha Latte Recipe: How to Make the Perfect Cup (Hot & Iced)
item.description: A foolproof matcha latte recipe — hot or iced — with the exact matcha-to-water ratio, the anti-clump technique, and 5 easy variations. No fancy equipment needed.

item.title: Flat White vs Latte: What's the Difference?
item.description: A flat white is smaller (5-6 oz) with a stronger espresso flavor and velvety microfoam. A latte is larger (8-12 oz) and creamier. Here's exactly how they differ.

item.title: Macchiato vs Latte: What's the Difference? (All Three Types Explained)
item.description: A macchiato is a tiny 2–3 oz espresso stained with a dot of foam. A latte is 8–12 oz with lots of steamed milk. Here's how traditional macchiatos, latte macchiatos, and lattes actually differ.

item.title: Cappuccino vs Latte: What's the Difference?
item.description: Cappuccino has thick foam and a stronger espresso flavor. Latte is larger and creamier with a thin layer of microfoam. Learn the differences in ratio, texture, taste, and how to make each.

item.title: Latte Recipe: How to Make a Latte at Home
item.description: Make a café-quality latte at home — the exact recipe, milk steaming technique, and tips for getting smooth microfoam every time.
